title: abies religiosa subsp. mexicana
intro: Abies religiosa subsp. mexicana, also known as the Mexican fir or Oaxacan fir, is a subspecies of fir tree native to the mountainous regions of Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, derived from its essential oils and resin. The health benefits of Abies religiosa subsp. mexicana include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. The plant can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and ointments, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
